gold_standard.description: Gold standard for evaluating a RAG system on English Wikipedia articles (topic: literature and literary movements). Contains definition questions, sections, categories, content search, metadata, counting, existence, listings, and relationships between articles. All answers computed from data.

questions.question: What is Dolce Stil Novo?
questions.answer: Dolce Stil Novo (Italian pronunciation: [ˈdoltʃe ˌstil ˈnɔːvo], 'sweet new style') was a literary movement in 13th and 14th century Italy.

questions.question: Define the concept of Intelligence literature.
questions.answer: Intelligence literature, sometimes referred to as espionage nonfiction, is a genre of non-fiction or historical literature, written in any language, that focuses on the intelligence field, and its most popular subfield known as espionage.

questions.question: What is the article about Progressive Writers' Movement about?
questions.answer: The Progressive Writers' Association or the Progressive Writers' Movement of India or Anjuman Tarraqi Pasand Mussanafin-e-Hind (Urdu:  انجمن ترقی پسند مصنفینِ ہند) or Akhil Bhartiya Pragatishil Lekhak Sangh (Hindi: अखिल भारतीय प्रगतिशील लेखक संघ) was a progressive literary movement in pre-partition British India.

questions.question: Briefly explain what Indianism (arts) is.
questions.answer: Indianism (Portuguese: Indianismo) was a Brazilian literary and artistic movement that reached its peak during the first stages of Romanticism in the country, though it had been present in Brazilian literature since the Baroque period.

questions.question: Give me a description of Second Sophistic.
questions.answer: The Second Sophistic is a literary-historical term referring to the Greek writers who flourished from the reign of Nero until c.

questions.question: How is Costumbrismo defined according to available information?
questions.answer: Costumbrismo (in Catalan: costumisme; sometimes anglicized as costumbrism, with the adjectival form costumbrist)  is the literary or pictorial interpretation of local everyday life, mannerisms, and customs, primarily in the Hispanic scene, and particularly in the 19th century, i.e.

questions.question: I need to know what Acmeist poetry is. Can you explain?
questions.answer: Acmeism, or the Guild of Poets, was a modernist transient poetic school, which emerged c.

questions.question: Summarize the topic of Romanticism in one sentence.
questions.answer: Romanticism (also known as the Romantic movement or Romantic era) was an artistic and intellectual movement that originated in Europe towards the end of the 18th century.

questions.question: What concept does the term Film adaptation designate?
questions.answer: A film adaptation transfers the details or story of an existing source text, such as a novel, into a feature film.

questions.question: What does Natural school consist of?
questions.answer: The natural school (Russian: Натуральная школа, romanized: Naturalnaya shkola) was a literary movement that arose under the influence of Nikolai Gogol in the 1840s up to the 1850s in Russia, and included such diverse authors as Nikolai Nekrasov, Ivan Goncharov, Ivan Turgenev, Mikhail Saltykov-Shchedrin, Ivan Panayev, Dmitry Grigorovich, Alexander Hertzen, Aleksey Pisemsky, Vladimir Dal, Fyodor Dostoyevsky and Evgeny Grebyonka, among others.

questions.question: Provide the definition of Bizarro fiction.
questions.answer: Bizarro fiction is a contemporary literary genre which often uses elements of absurdism, satire, and the grotesque, along with pop-surrealism and genre fiction staples, in order to create subversive, weird, and entertaining works.

questions.question: What does the concept of Colon (rhetoric) refer to?
questions.answer: A colon (from Greek: κῶλον, pl.

questions.question: Tell me about Unanimism. What is it exactly?
questions.answer: Unanimism (French: unanimisme) is a movement in French literature begun by Jules Romains in the early 1900s, with his first book, La vie unanime, published in 1904.

questions.question: What is the meaning of Generation Kindle?
questions.answer: The term Generation Kindle (in Spanish: Generación Kindle) refers to authors who publish their works digitally through Kindle Direct Publishing (KDP) for (Amazon.com).

questions.question: If I look up Parnassianism, what information do I find?
questions.answer: Parnassianism (or Parnassism) was a group of French poets that began during the positivist period of the 19th century (1860s–1890s), occurring after romanticism and prior to symbolism.

questions.question: Could you tell me what Atticism is in a concise manner?
questions.answer: Atticism (meaning "favouring Attica", the region of Athens in Greece) was a rhetorical movement that began in the first quarter of the 1st century BC.

questions.question: Describe the concept covered under the name Poetic tradition.
questions.answer: Poetic tradition is a concept similar to that of the poetic or literary canon (a body of works of significant literary merit, instrumental in shaping Western culture and modes of thought).

questions.question: I want to understand what Epigram is. Summarize it.
questions.answer: An epigram is a brief, interesting, memorable, sometimes surprising or satirical statement.

questions.question: What information is available about Lyrics?
questions.answer: Lyrics are words that make up a song, usually consisting of verses and choruses.

questions.question: Explain the topic of Irrealism (the arts) in a few words.
questions.answer: Irrealism is a term that has been used by various writers in the fields of philosophy, literature, and art to denote specific modes of unreality and/or the problems in concretely defining reality.

questions.question: What do we understand by Latin American poetry?
questions.answer: Latin American poetry is the poetry written by Latin American authors.

questions.question: Talk to me about Signalism.
questions.answer: Signalism (Serbo-Croatian: Сигнализам, Signalizam; from Latin: signum, lit.

questions.question: I am interested in knowing what Interlingue literature is. Give me an introduction.
questions.answer: Interlingue literature broadly encompasses the body of fiction and nonfiction work created or translated into Interlingue, a constructed language created by Edgar de Wahl.

questions.question: What can you tell me about Film-poem?
questions.answer: The film-poem (also called the poetic avant-garde film, verse-film  or verse-documentary or film poem without the hyphen)  is a label first applied to American avant-garde films released after World War II.

questions.question: How would you define Rhyming Chroniclers based on the data?
questions.answer: Rhyming Chroniclers, a series of writers who flourished in England in the 13th century, and related histories of the country in rhyme, in which the fabulous occupies a conspicuous place, among which Layamon's Brut (1205) takes the lead.

questions.question: What is known about List of literary movements?
questions.answer: Literary movements are a way to divide literature into categories of similar philosophical, topical, or aesthetic features, as opposed to divisions by genre or period.

questions.question: Do you have information about Latin American Boom? Summarize it.
questions.answer: The Latin American Boom (Spanish: Boom latinoamericano) was a literary movement of the 1960s and 1970s when the work of a group of relatively young Latin American novelists became widely circulated in Europe and throughout the world.

questions.question: Give a brief summary of what Committed literature is.
questions.answer: Committed literature (French: littérature engagée) can be defined as an approach of an author, poet, novelist, playwright or composer who commits their work to defend or assert an ethical, political, social, ideological or religious view, most often through their works but also can loosely be defined as being through their direct intervention as an "intellectual", in public affairs (Crowly, 2018).

questions.question: I would like you to clarify what Young Vienna is.
questions.answer: Young Vienna (Jung-Wien), also referred to as Young Austria, was a society of fin de siècle writers who met in Vienna's Café Griensteidl and other nearby coffeehouses in the late nineteenth century.

questions.question: In what context does Offbeat generation appear and what does it mean?
questions.answer: The Offbeat generation was a loose association of like-minded writers working across different styles but united by their opposition to a mainstream publishing industry driven by marketing departments.
